1189	  /// Did the responder consent to having their response published if
1190	  /// the firm decides to reply publicly? Captured per-response on the
1191	  /// submission form (Round 17 prompt 31). When false, the founder
1192	  /// queue suppresses the "Promote to PublicReply" affordance and the
1193	  /// public-article appendix never renders this row.
1194	  publishConsent Boolean @default(false)
1195	
1196	  /// pending | approved | rejected | engaged | archived
1197	  status        String @default("pending")
1198	  moderatorNote String @default("")
1199	
1200	  createdAt DateTime  @default(now())
1201	  seenAt    DateTime?
1202	
1203	  organization Organization        @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
1204	  published    PublishedConclusion @relation(fields: [publishedConclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
1205	  triage       ResponseTriage?
1206	  publicReply  PublicReply?
1207	
1208	  @@index([organizationId])
1209	  @@index([publishedConclusionId])
1210	  @@index([status])
1211	  @@index([seenAt])
1212	}
1213	
1214	// ── Round 17 prompt 31: response triage + dialog loop ──────────────────────
1215	//
1216	// `ResponseTriage` is a 1:1 sidecar to `PublicResponse` written by the
1217	// Python classifier in `noosphere/literature/response_triage.py`. The
1218	// triage row is the queue's source of priority (substantive vs noise);
1219	// the founder retains the override via `manualLabel` / `manualReason`.
1220	//
1221	// The plaintext submitter email is NEVER duplicated here; we only keep
1222	// the SHA-256 hash so the queue can flag repeat-spam senders without
1223	// expanding the PII surface.
1224	model ResponseTriage {
1225	  id               String @id @default(cuid())
1226	  organizationId   String
1227	  publicResponseId String @unique
1228	
1229	  /// SUBSTANTIVE_OBJECTION | CLARIFICATION_REQUEST | GENERAL_ENGAGEMENT | SPAM_NOISE
1230	  label      String
1231	  /// Founder override of `label`. Empty string = honour the classifier label.
1232	  manualLabel String @default("")
1233	
1234	  /// "" | too_short | promotional_link | off_topic | abusive_language | repeat_sender | low_information
1235	  spamReason String @default("")
1236	  /// Founder override of `spamReason` (audit trail; e.g. when un-spamming).
1237	  manualReason String @default("")
1238	
1239	  confidence  Float
1240	  /// One-sentence reduction of the implied objection. Empty unless
1241	  /// label == SUBSTANTIVE_OBJECTION. Feeds the severity rubric.
1242	  impliedObjection String @default("") @db.Text
1243	  rationale        String @default("") @db.Text
1244	  usedLlm          Boolean @default(false)
1245	
1246	  /// SHA-256 of the lowercased submitter email; "" for pseudonymous.
1247	  senderHash          String  @default("")
1248	  elevatedSenderFlag  Boolean @default(false)
1249	
1250	  /// Severity input snapshot from the cascade graph at triage time —
1251	  /// stored so the queue can sort by potential severity without a
1252	  /// fresh cascade traversal on every page render. JSON shape mirrors
1253	  /// `SeverityInputs` in `noosphere/peer_review/severity.py`.
1254	  severityInputsJson String @default("{}")
1255	  /// Cached numeric severity in [0, 1] derived from the snapshot.
1256	  severityValue      Float  @default(0)
1257	
1258	  createdAt DateTime  @default(now())
1259	  /// Set when the founder archives the row (spam path or
1260	  /// substantive-but-handled). Archived rows drop out of the queue.
1261	  archivedAt   DateTime?
1262	  archiveNote  String    @default("")
1263	
1264	  organization   Organization   @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
1265	  publicResponse PublicResponse @relation(fields: [publicResponseId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
1266	
1267	  @@index([organizationId])
1268	  @@index([organizationId, label])
1269	  @@index([organizationId, archivedAt, severityValue])
1270	  @@index([senderHash])
1271	}

model Organization {
  id        String    @id @default(cuid())
  /// URL-safe tenant key (login, subdomains, worker routing)
  slug      String    @unique
  name      String
  /// Soft-delete grace window (hard-delete after policy window)
  deletedAt DateTime?
  createdAt DateTime  @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime  @updatedAt

  founders                 Founder[]
  sessions                 Session[]
  uploads                  Upload[]
  conclusions              Conclusion[]
  contradictions           Contradiction[]
  driftEvents              DriftEvent[]
  researchSuggestions      ResearchSuggestion[]
  reviewItems              ReviewItem[]
  openQuestions            OpenQuestion[]
  auditEvents              AuditEvent[]
  publicationReviews       PublicationReview[]
  publishedConclusions     PublishedConclusion[]
  publicResponses          PublicResponse[]
  responseTriages          ResponseTriage[]
  publicReplies            PublicReply[]
  apiKeys                  ApiKey[]
  currentEvents            CurrentEvent[]
  eventOpinions            EventOpinion[]

   instance seeded from ``fixtures/minimal_codex_schema.sql`` so the

"""Follow-digest scheduler.

Runs the per-subscriber digest pipeline on a fixed cadence (weekly by
default; immediate for firm-wide major events; monthly for opted-in
subscribers). The scheduler does not own the subscriber list or the
mail transport — those live in the Next.js codex app, which is also
the system of record for double-opt-in confirms and one-click
unsubscribes.

The scheduler's job is to:

  1. Load a snapshot of active subscribers + recent digest-eligible
     events from a JSON intake (produced by the codex export).
  2. Hand both to ``digest_builder.build_digests`` to compute the
     per-subscriber payloads.
  3. Emit the rendered digests as a JSON outbox the codex app picks
     up and sends through the existing ``sendMail`` pipeline (Resend
     or SMTP). No third-party email vendor with non-auditable
     telemetry is used; receipts go through the same path as every
     other firm email.

Keeping the boundary at JSON intake/outbox means tests do not need a
live database: the pure event/subscriber types are sufficient.
"""

    if not value or not isinstance(value, str):

"""Follow-digest scheduler.

default; immediate for firm-wide major events; monthly for opted-in
subscribers). The scheduler does not own the subscriber list or the

the system of record for double-opt-in confirms and one-click
unsubscribes.

The scheduler's job is to:

  1. Load a snapshot of active subscribers + recent digest-eligible
     events from a JSON intake (produced by the codex export).
  2. Hand both to ``digest_builder.build_digests`` to compute the
     per-subscriber payloads.

     up and sends through the existing ``sendMail`` pipeline (Resend
     or SMTP). No third-party email vendor with non-auditable
     telemetry is used; receipts go through the same path as every
     other firm email.

live database: the pure event/subscriber types are sufficient.

"""Tests for the public follow-digest builder + scheduler.

The codex-side double-opt-in flow and one-click unsubscribe are
exercised by Vitest tests in ``theseus-codex/src/lib`` against the real
Prisma client; this Python suite focuses on the per-subscriber
selection, scope-matching, cadence windowing, and digest rendering
that lives in ``noosphere.social.digest_builder``.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from pathlib import Path

from noosphere.social import digest_builder as db
from noosphere.social import scheduler as sch

NOW = datetime(2026, 5, 8, 12, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
SITE = "https://theseus.test"


def _sub(
    *,
    sid: str = "sub_a",
    email: str = "reader@example.org",
    scope: db.Scope = "firm",
    scope_key: str = "",
    cadence: db.Cadence = "weekly",
    last_sent_at: datetime | None = None,
    token: str = "tok-a",
) -> db.Subscriber:
    return db.Subscriber(
        id=sid,
        email=email,
        scope=scope,
        scope_key=scope_key,
        cadence=cadence,
        unsubscribe_token=token,
        last_sent_at=last_sent_at,
    )


def _event(
    kind: db.EventKind,
    *,
    headline: str = "Headline",
    summary: str = "Summary",
    url: str = "https://theseus.test/c/example/v/1",
    occurred_at: datetime | None = None,
    conclusion_slug: str = "example",
    methodology_names: tuple[str, ...] = (),
    domain_tags: tuple[str, ...] = (),
    is_major: bool = False,
) -> db.DigestEvent:
    return db.DigestEvent(
        kind=kind,
        headline=headline,
        summary=summary,
        url=url,
        occurred_at=occurred_at or (NOW - timedelta(hours=1)),
        conclusion_slug=conclusion_slug,
        methodology_names=methodology_names,
        domain_tags=domain_tags,
        is_major=is_major,
    )


# ── scope filtering ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def test_firm_scope_matches_every_event() -> None:
    sub = _sub(scope="firm")
    pub = _event("publication")
    rev = _event("revision", kind_marker="revision") if False else _event("revision")
    items = db.select_events_for(sub, [pub, rev], NOW)
    assert {e.kind for e in items} == {"publication", "revision"}


def test_methodology_scope_matches_only_events_tagged_with_method() -> None:
    sub = _sub(scope="methodology", scope_key="six_layer_coherence")
    e1 = _event("publication", methodology_names=("six_layer_coherence",))
    e2 = _event(
        "publication",
        methodology_names=("contradiction_probe",),
        conclusion_slug="other",
    )
    items = db.select_events_for(sub, [e1, e2], NOW)
    assert [e.conclusion_slug for e in items] == ["example"]


def test_domain_scope_is_case_insensitive() -> None:
    sub = _sub(scope="domain", scope_key="ML-Safety")
    in_scope = _event("publication", domain_tags=("ml-safety",))
    out_of_scope = _event("publication", domain_tags=("biology",))
    items = db.select_events_for(sub, [in_scope, out_of_scope], NOW)
    assert items == [in_scope]


def test_conclusion_scope_uses_exact_slug() -> None:

    export must filter on ``status='active'`` before calling.

content.text: Done. Summary of what landed: **Schema** (`theseus-codex/prisma/schema.prisma`) — Added `Subscriber` model with `(organizationId, email, scope, scopeKey)` uniqueness, `pending|active|unsubscribed` status, double-opt-in `confirmToken`, unique `unsubscribeToken`, `cadence` (weekly/immediate/monthly), `lastSentAt`, and an unsubscribe-reason field. Wired the relation into `Organization`. **Subscriptions library** (`src/lib/subscriptions.ts`) — Email validation, scope-key normalization, token generation (32-byte base64url), `createOrReviveSubscriber` / `confirmSubscriber` / `unsubscribeByToken`, and the confirm-email builder. No tracking pixels, includes `List-Unsubscribe` headers. **API routes**: - `POST /api/public/subscribe` — Validates, rate-limits per email/24h, sends double-opt-in confirm email. - `GET /api/public/subscribe/confirm?token=…` — Flips `pending → active`. - `GET|POST /api/public/unsubscribe/[token]` — One-click; POST captures optional free-text reason. **Component** (`src/components/SubscribeForm.tsx`) — Client component, scope-typed (firm/methodology/domain/conclusion), cadence picker, posts to the subscribe endpoint; embedded on the method overview page (`/methodology/[method]`) and the homepage (`src/app/page.tsx` — there is no `(home)/page.tsx` in this repo; the `(home)` directory holds nested components imported by the root `page.tsx`). **Noosphere side**: - `noosphere/social/digest_builder.py` — Pure dataclasses (`Subscriber`, `DigestEvent`, `Digest`), scope filtering, cadence windowing (7d weekly / 30d monthly / `is_major`-only immediate), `last_sent_at` clipping, and text+HTML rendering that gives revisions/retractions the same prominence as new publications. - `noosphere/social/scheduler.py` — JSON intake → `build_digests` → JSON outbox; the codex app does the actual mailer call via the existing `sendMail` (Resend/SMTP) path. CLI: `python -m noosphere.social.scheduler --intake … --outbox …`. **Tests** (`noosphere/tests/test_digest_builder.py`) — 14 tests, all green: scope filtering across all four scopes, cadence windowing, `last_sent_at` clipping, immediate-only-major rule, no ``/iframe/1x1/beacon in HTML, unsubscribe-cycle invariant, intake/outbox round-trip, and the double-opt-in contract boundary. Prisma `generate` + `validate` both pass; `tsc --noEmit` shows no new type errors (one pre-existing error in `ops/page.tsx` is unrelated).
